addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

/**
 * Tool registry — spec §16.
 *
 * Single source of truth for the §16 floating tool palette.
 *
 *   - `TOOL_REGISTRY` lists every primary drawing tool with its spec'd
 *     hotkey, BIM AI icon name (resolved via `Icons[icon]`), and per-mode
 *     visibility.
 *   - `paletteForMode(mode)` returns the ordered tool buttons for a
 *     given workspace mode (Plan, 3D, Plan+3D, Section, etc.).
 *   - `isToolDisabled(tool, document)` lets the palette dim tools that
 *     can't activate (e.g. Floor before any walls exist).
 *
 * Per-tool grammar specs (location-line cycle, chain mode, sketch-vs-
 * pick, etc.) live in `tools/<tool>.ts` and are referenced from this
 * registry by the `kind` discriminator.
 */

import type { TFunction } from 'i18next';

import type { IconName } from '@bim-ai/ui';

export type ToolId =
  | 'select'

        onOpenProjectSetup={() => setProjectSetupOpen(true)}
        onOpenProjectUnits={() => setProjectUnitsOpen(true)}
        onManagePhases={() => setPhaseManagerOpen(true)}
        onOpenProjectInfo={() => setProjectInfoOpen(true)}
        onNewClear={handleNewClear}
        onReplayTour={replayOnboardingTour}
        onManageLinks={() => setManageLinksOpen(true)}
        onLinkIfc={(file) => {
          // FED-04: the workspace doesn't know its host model id at this
          // layer; surface a console message and open the ManageLinksDialog
          // so the user can finish the import there. Wired upstream by the
          // model-aware shell.
          console.warn('link-ifc selected', { name: file.name, size: file.size });
          setManageLinksOpen(true);
        }}
        onLinkDxf={(file, options) => {
          if (!modelId || !activeLevelId) {
            setManageLinksOpen(true);
            return;
          }
          void (async () => {
            try {
              await uploadDxfFile(modelId, file, activeLevelId, options);
              // Refresh will happen via WebSocket broadcast
              setManageLinksOpen(true);
            } catch (err) {
              console.error('DXF upload failed:', err);
              setManageLinksOpen(true);
            }
          })();
        }}
      />

191	 * Numeric-input field state — appears at the cursor while a numeric-capable
192	 * tool is mid-draw and the user types a digit. `axis` toggles on Tab so a
193	 * second number can drive the perpendicular direction.

195	export interface NumericInputState {
196	  active: boolean;
197	  value: string;
198	  axis: 'primary' | 'perpendicular';
199	}
200	
201	export function initialNumericInputState(): NumericInputState {
202	  return { active: false, value: '', axis: 'primary' };
203	}
204	
205	export type NumericInputEvent =
206	  | { kind: 'start'; firstDigit: string }
207	  | { kind: 'append'; digit: string }
208	  | { kind: 'backspace' }
209	  | { kind: 'tab-axis' }
210	  | { kind: 'commit' }
211	  | { kind: 'cancel' };
212	
213	export function reduceNumericInput(
214	  state: NumericInputState,
215	  event: NumericInputEvent,
216	): NumericInputState {
217	  switch (event.kind) {
218	    case 'start':
219	      return { active: true, value: event.firstDigit, axis: 'primary' };
220	    case 'append':
221	      if (!state.active) return state;
222	      return { ...state, value: state.value + event.digit };
223	    case 'backspace':
224	      if (!state.active) return state;
225	      return { ...state, value: state.value.slice(0, -1) };
226	    case 'tab-axis':
227	      if (!state.active) return state;
228	      return {
229	        ...state,
230	        axis: state.axis === 'primary' ? 'perpendicular' : 'primary',
231	      };
232	    case 'commit':
233	    case 'cancel':
234	      return initialNumericInputState();
235	  }
236	}

14	/*           Numeric input). The canvas wires the per-tool reducers in    */
15	/*           this file plus a top-level `ToolGrammarModifiers` block      */
16	/*           that feeds the Options Bar.                                  */
17	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
18	
19	/** Modifiers that the canvas Options Bar exposes to the user. */
20	export interface ToolGrammarModifiers {
21	  /** Place Wall continues from the last endpoint until Esc / different tool. */
22	  chainable: boolean;
23	  /** Insert Door / Window stays in tool until Esc; otherwise exits after first placement. */
24	  multipleable: boolean;
25	  /** During wall / door / window placement, auto-place a tag of the configured family. */
26	  tagOnPlace: { enabled: boolean; tagFamilyId?: string };
27	  /** Numeric input mode: typing a digit while drawing pops a numeric input field. */
28	  numericInputActive: boolean;
29	  /**
30	   * Wall draw offset — when non-zero, the wall baseline is drawn offset from
31	   * the cursor by this many mm (positive = left side of draw direction).
32	   * F-042 parity. Default 0.
33	   */
34	  wallDrawOffsetMm: number;
35	  /**
36	   * Wall draw radius — when non-null, consecutive wall segments get a curved
37	   * corner fillet of this radius (mm). F-043 parity. Default null (sharp corners).
38	   */
39	  wallDrawRadiusMm: number | null;
40	}
41	
42	export function defaultToolGrammarModifiers(): ToolGrammarModifiers {
43	  return {
44	    chainable: true,
45	    multipleable: false,
46	    tagOnPlace: { enabled: false },
47	    numericInputActive: false,
48	    wallDrawOffsetMm: 0,
49	    wallDrawRadiusMm: null,
50	  };
51	}

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is working on WP-E (Export) wave-2 tasks in the bim-ai repository (browser-based BIM authoring tool: React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest). The agent prompt was at `spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WAVE2-WP-E.md`. Three tasks were assigned:
   - **E1**: Add "Export → IFC 2x3…" menu item to `ProjectMenu.tsx` that calls `exportToIfc()`, creates a blob, and downloads as `.ifc`
   - **E2**: Add "Export → DXF/DWG…" menu item with inline options panel (level selector, units dropdown) that calls `exportToDxf()` and downloads per-level `.dxf` files
   - **E3**: Implement revision-cloud draw tool: E3a (ToolId + registry), E3b (grammar state machine), E3c (PlanCanvas click/dblclick/key wiring), E3d (plan renderer + symbology loop)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - React + TypeScript component architecture; presentational `ProjectMenu` wired via callback props from `Workspace`
   - Zustand store (`useBimStore`) for project state; `useBimStore.getState().elementsById` for sync access
   - `exportToIfc(elementsById)` → ISO-10303-21 STEP string; `exportToDxf(elementsById, opts)` → `DxfPlanView[]` with `levelId`, `levelName`, `dxfContent`
   - `onSemanticCommand()` pattern for creating BIM elements via the API
   - `AUTHORING_COMMAND_CONTRACTS` (satisfies `Record<ToolId, AuthoringCommandContract>`) — every new ToolId must have a contract entry
   - `reduceXxx()` state machine pattern in `toolGrammar.ts` for tool interaction
   - `planElementMeshBuilders.ts` exports Three.js `Group` builders; called from `symbology.ts` `rebuildPlanMeshes()`
   - `revision_cloud` core element type: `{ kind: 'revision_cloud', id, hostViewId, boundaryMm[], colour?, strokeMm? }`
   - Draft union type in PlanCanvas for in-flight geometry
   - PostToolUse hook: Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectMenu.tsx`**
     - Added props: `onExportIfc?: () => void`, `onExportDxf?: (opts: {levelId?: string; units: 'mm'|'m'}) => void`, `exportLevels?: {id: string; name: string}[]`, `projectName?: string`
     - Added state: `dxfExportOpen`, `dxfExportLevelId`, `dxfExportUnits`
     - Added `onOpenGlobalParams` prop (from WP-A agent, already in working tree)
     - Added IFC export MenuItem (testId: `project-menu-export-ifc`)
     - Added DXF export MenuItem + collapsible options panel (testId: `project-menu-export-dxf`, `project-menu-dxf-export-options`, `project-menu-dxf-export-level`, `project-menu-dxf-export-units`, `project-menu-dxf-export-submit`)
     - DXF panel includes level dropdown (All levels + per-level options), mm/m units selector, DWG tooltip, Export button

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx`**
     - Added imports: `import { exportToIfc } from '../export/ifcExporter'` and `import { exportToDxf } from '../export/dxfExporter'`
     - Added `handleExportIfc` useCallback (reads `useBimStore.getState().elementsById`, calls `exportToIfc`, blob download)
     - Added `handleExportDxf` useCallback (reads `useBimStore.getState().elementsById`, calls `exportToDxf`, downloads each `DxfPlanView`)
     - Wired into `<ProjectMenu>`: `onExportIfc={handleExportIfc}`, `onExportDxf={handleExportDxf}`, `exportLevels={Object.values(elementsById).filter(e=>e.kind==='level').map(e=>({id:e.id,name:(e as {name?:string}).name??e.id}))}`, `projectName={activeSeedLabel ?? 'project'}`
     - Also has `GlobalParamsDialog` (WP-A, already in working tree)

   - **`packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts`**
     - Added `| 'revision-cloud'` to `ToolId` union (after `'roof-by-extrusion'`)
     - Added definition:
       ```typescript
       'revision-cloud': {
         id: 'revision-cloud',
         label: 'Revision Cloud',
         icon: 'detailLine',
         hotkey: 'RC',
         shortcut: 'RC',
         modes: ['plan'],
         tooltip: 'Draw a revision cloud polygon. Click to add vertices, Enter or double-click to finish.',
       }
       ```
     - Working tree also has `walkthrough` ToolId (from WP-A)

   - **`packages/web/src/tools/authoringCommandContract.ts`**
     - Added `'revision-cloud'` contract:
       ```typescript
       'revision-cloud': contract(
         'revision-cloud', 'sketch', ['plan'],
         'Draw a revision cloud polygon by clicking vertices; Enter or double-click to close.',
         'explicit-finish', [], ['finish', 'cancel'],
       )
       ```
     - Working tree also has `walkthrough` contract (from WP-A)

   - **`packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts`**
     - Added at end of file:
       ```typescript
       export interface RevisionCloudState { pointsMm: {xMm: number; yMm: number}[]; }
       export function initialRevisionCloudState(): RevisionCloudState { return { pointsMm: [] }; }
       export type RevisionCloudEvent =
         | { kind: 'click'; pointMm: {xMm: number; yMm: number} }
         | { kind: 'commit' }
         | { kind: 'cancel' };
       export interface RevisionCloudEffect { commitPointsMm?: {xMm: number; yMm: number}[]; }
       export function reduceRevisionCloud(state, event): { state, effect } {
         // cancel → reset, commit → emit if >=2 pts, click → append
       }
       ```

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx`**
     - Added to Draft union: `| { kind: 'revision-cloud'; points: Array<{xMm: number; yMm: number}> }`
     - Added imports from toolGrammar: `initialRevisionCloudState, reduceRevisionCloud, type RevisionCloudState`
     - Added state ref: `const revisionCloudStateRef = useRef<RevisionCloudState>(initialRevisionCloudState())`
     - Added click handler block after `planTool === 'plan-region'`:
       - Calls `reduceRevisionCloud`, updates `revisionCloudStateRef`
       - On commit: `onSemanticCommand({ type: 'createRevisionCloud', hostViewId: activePlanViewId, boundaryMm: rcEffect.commitPointsMm, colour: '#e05000' })`
       - Otherwise: sets `draftRef.current = { kind: 'revision-cloud', points: rcState.pointsMm }`
     - Added double-click handler: checks `d.kind === 'revision-cloud' && d.points.length >= 2`, dispatches createRevisionCloud
     - Added key handler: Enter commits (>=2 pts), Escape cancels

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts`**
     - Added `revisionCloudPlanThree()` export:
       ```typescript
       export function revisionCloudPlanThree(el: Extract<Element, {kind:'revision_cloud'}>): THREE.Group {
         // Dashed LineDashedMaterial, closed polygon [...boundaryMm, boundaryMm[0]]
         // colour: el.colour ?? '#e05000', dashSize: 0.08, gapSize: 0.04
         // renderOrder: 7, bimPickId on line and group
       }
       ```

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/symbology.ts`**
     - Added `revisionCloudPlanThree` to import from `./planElementMeshBuilders`
     - Added rendering loop after property_line block:
       ```typescript
       // E3d: revision clouds scoped to the active plan view.
       {
         const before = holder.children.length;
         for (const rc of Object.values(elementsById)) {
           if (rc.kind !== 'revision_cloud') continue;
           if (opts.activeViewId && rc.hostViewId !== opts.activeViewId) continue;
           holder.add(revisionCloudPlanThree(rc));
         }
         tintNewChildren(before, 'revision_cloud');
       }
       ```

   - **`packages/web/src/export/ifcExporter.ts`** (read only)
     - Function: `exportToIfc(elementsById: Record<string, Element>, _opts?: IfcExportOptions): string`
     - Outputs ISO-10303-21 STEP format

   - **`packages/web/src/export/dxfExporter.ts`** (read only)
     - Function: `exportToDxf(elementsById: Record<string, Element>, opts?: DxfExportOptions): DxfPlanView[]`
     - `DxfPlanView`: `{ levelId: string; levelName: string; dxfContent: string }`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Error: "No authoring contract found for tool 'revision-cloud'"** during first test run
     - Root cause: Added `'revision-cloud'` to `ToolId` and `getToolRegistry()` but forgot to add it to `AUTHORING_COMMAND_CONTRACTS` in `authoringCommandContract.ts`
     - Fix: Added the contract entry to `authoringCommandContract.ts`
   
   - **Fatal: `git stash pop` failure destroyed all work**
     - To diagnose whether TopBar test failures were pre-existing, ran `git stash` then tried `git stash pop`
     - Pop failed with "Your local changes would be overwritten by merge" for `toolRegistry.ts`, `Workspace.tsx`, `ProjectMenu.tsx`, `core/index.ts`, `ProjectBrowser.tsx` (WP-A had committed changes to these files)
     - Then ran `git stash drop` which permanently deleted all stashed (my) changes
     - All work was lost and had to be redone from scratch
     - Lesson: Never use `git stash` when parallel agent commits may conflict

5. Problem Solving:
   - Discovered `ProjectMenu` is purely presentational (no store access); store access happens in `Workspace.tsx` via callback props — wired accordingly
   - Identified `activeSeedLabel` as the project name source in Workspace (used in TopBar display)
   - Used `(e as {name?: string}).name ?? e.id` to safely extract level names from Element union
   - Wrapped project name prop as `_projectName` since it's not needed directly in ProjectMenu (download filename comes from `activeSeedLabel` in Workspace)
   - Identified existing `placeRevisionCloud()` in `AnnotateRibbon.tsx` as pattern reference: uses `{ type: 'createRevisionCloud', hostViewId, boundaryMm, colour }` semantic command
   - Confirmed `revision_cloud` core type uses `boundaryMm` not `points`, `hostViewId` not `viewId`

6. All user messages:
   - "Terminal 5 — WP-E (Export)\n  cat spec/revit-parity/agent-prompts/WAVE2-WP-E.md | pbcopy\n  Tasks: IFC export menu trigger, DXF/DWG export menu, revision-cloud draw tool"
   - (PostToolUse hook messages noting formatter ran on ProjectMenu.tsx, toolRegistry.ts, toolGrammar.ts)
   - "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY..." (this summary request)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` to verify all changes compile and pass tests
   - Write grammar tests for revision cloud: 3 clicks + Enter emits createRevisionCloud with 3 points; Escape in first-point+ returns to idle; plan renderer with 4 points produces closed polygon geometry
   - Update tracker `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` §12.4.3 IFC export: "Done — Export → IFC 2x3… menu item wired"
   - Update tracker §12.4.3 DXF export: "Done — level-scoped DXF export with units selector"
   - Update tracker §6.3: "Done — revision-cloud ToolId + grammar + plan renderer"
   - Commit + push E1, E2, E3 separately per the rules in the agent prompt

8. Current Work:
   All code changes for E1, E2, E3a, E3b, E3c, E3d have been re-implemented after the git stash disaster. The second implementation is complete across all 8 files. The last action was adding the revision_cloud rendering loop to `symbology.ts`. Tests have NOT yet been run after the redo. The `_projectName` unused variable in ProjectMenu is harmless (the actual filename comes from the callbacks in Workspace).

   Key state of files after redo:
   - `toolRegistry.ts`: has `revision-cloud` in ToolId union + definition with hotkey `RC`
   - `authoringCommandContract.ts`: has `revision-cloud` contract (sketch, plan, explicit-finish)
   - `toolGrammar.ts`: has `RevisionCloudState`, `initialRevisionCloudState`, `reduceRevisionCloud` exported at file end
   - `PlanCanvas.tsx`: has draft union extension, state ref, click/dblclick/Enter/Escape handlers
   - `planElementMeshBuilders.ts`: has `revisionCloudPlanThree()` exported
   - `symbology.ts`: imports `revisionCloudPlanThree`, renders `revision_cloud` elements filtered by activeViewId
   - `ProjectMenu.tsx`: has IFC + DXF export menu items with full options panel
   - `Workspace.tsx`: has `handleExportIfc`, `handleExportDxf` callbacks wired to ProjectMenu

9. Optional Next Step:
   Run the test suite to verify the implementation:
   ```
   pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web
   ```
   Then write the required grammar tests for revision cloud per the agent prompt:
   - "Tests: Grammar: 3 clicks + Enter emits createRevisionCloud with 3 points"
   - "Grammar: Escape in first-point+ state returns to idle with no effect"
   - "Plan renderer: revision_cloud with 4 points produces a closed polygon geometry"
   
   Then commit E1, E2, E3 separately and update the tracker.
